author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
Again on Matt. xiv. 25: Of the Lord walking on the waves of the sea, and of Peter tottering.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: Again on Matt. xiv. 25: Of the Lord walking on the waves of the sea, and of Peter tottering.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Gospel which has just been read touching the Lord Christ, who walked on the waters of the sea; and the Apostle Peter, who as he was walking, tottered through fear, and sinking in distrust, rose again by confession, gives us to understand that the sea is the present world, and the Apostle Peter the type of the One Church.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For Peter in the order of Apostles first, and in the love of Christ most forward, answers oftentimes alone for all the rest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Again, when the Lord Jesus Christ asked, whom men said that He was, and when the disciples gave the various opinions of men, and the Lord asked again and said, “But whom say ye that I am?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Peter answered, “Thou art the Christ, the Son of the living God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: One for many gave the answer, Unity in many.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then said the Lord to Him, “Blessed art thou, Simon Barjonas: for flesh and blood hath not revealed it unto thee, but My Father which is in heaven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then He added, “and I say unto thee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As if He had said, “Because thou hast said unto Me, ‘Thou art the Christ the Son of the living God;’ I also say unto thee, ‘Thou art Peter.’”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For before he was called Simon.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now this name of Peter was given him by the Lord, and that in a figure, that he should signify the Church.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For seeing that Christ is the rock (Petra), Peter is the Christian people.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the rock (Petra) is the original name.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore Peter is so called from the rock; not the rock from Peter; as Christ is not called Christ from the Christian, but the Christian from Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Therefore,” he saith, “Thou art Peter; and upon this Rock” which thou hast confessed, upon this Rock which thou hast acknowledged, saying, “Thou art the Christ, the Son of the living God, will I build My Church;” that is upon Myself, the Son of the living God, “will I build My Church.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I will build thee upon Myself, not Myself upon thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For men who wished to be built upon men, said, “I am of Paul; and I of Apollos; and I of Cephas,” who is Peter.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But others who did not wish to be built upon Peter, but upon the Rock, said, “But I am of Christ.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And when the Apostle Paul ascertained that he was chosen, and Christ despised, he said, “Is Christ divided? was Paul crucified for you? or were ye baptized in the name of Paul?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And, as not in the name of Paul, so neither in the name of Peter; but in the name of Christ: that Peter might be built upon the Rock, not the Rock upon Peter.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This same Peter therefore who had been by the Rock pronounced “blessed,” bearing the figure of the Church, holding the chief place in the Apostleship, a very little while after that he had heard that he was “blessed,” a very little while after that he had heard that he was “Peter,” a very little while after that he had heard that he was to be “built upon the Rock,” displeased the Lord when He had heard of His future Passion, for He had foretold His disciples that it was soon to be.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He feared lest he should by death, lose Him whom he had confessed as the fountain of life.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He was troubled, and said, “Be it far from Thee, Lord: this shall not be to Thee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Spare Thyself, O God, I am not willing that Thou shouldest die.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Peter said to Christ, I am not willing that Thou shouldest die; but Christ far better said, I am willing to die for thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And then He forthwith rebuked him, whom He had a little before commended; and calleth him Satan, whom he had pronounced “blessed.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Get thee behind Me, Satan,” he saith, “thou art an offence unto Me: for thou savourest not the things that be of God, but those that be of men.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What would He have us do in our present state, who thus findeth fault because we are men?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Would you know what He would have us do?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Give ear to the Psalm; “I have said, Ye are gods, and ye are all the children of the Most High.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But by savouring the things of men; “ye shall die like men.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The very same Peter a little while before blessed, afterwards Satan, in one moment, within a few words!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou wonderest at the difference of the names, mark the difference of the reasons of them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why wonderest thou that he who was a little before blessed, is afterwards Satan?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Mark the reason wherefore he is blessed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Because flesh and blood hath not revealed it unto thee, but My Father which is in heaven.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore blessed, because flesh and blood hath not revealed it unto thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if flesh and blood revealed this to thee, it were of thine own; but because flesh and blood hath not revealed it unto thee, but My Father which is in heaven, it is of Mine, not of thine own.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why of Mine?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Because all things that the Father hath are Mine.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then thou hast heard the cause, why he is “blessed,” and why he is “Peter.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But why was he that which we shudder at, and are loth to repeat, why, but because it was of thine own?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For thou savourest not the things which be of God, but those that be of men.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us, looking at ourselves in this member of the Church, distinguish what is of God, and what of ourselves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For then we shall not totter, then shall we be founded on the Rock, shall be fixed and firm against the winds, and storms, and streams, the temptations, I mean, of this present world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet see this Peter, who was then our figure; now he trusts, and now he totters; now he confesses the Undying, and now he fears lest He should die.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wherefore? because the Church of Christ hath both strong and weak ones; and cannot be without either strong or weak; whence the Apostle Paul says, “Now we that are strong ought to bear the infirmities of the weak.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In that Peter said, “Thou art the Christ, the Son of the living God,” he represents the strong: but in that he totters, and would not that Christ should suffer, in fearing death for Him, and not acknowledging the Life, he represents the weak ones of the Church.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In that one Apostle then, that is, Peter, in the order of Apostles first and chiefest, in whom the Church was figured, both sorts were to be represented, that is, both the strong and weak; because the Church doth not exist without them both.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And hence also is that which was just now read, “Lord, if it be Thou, bid me come unto Thee on the water.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For I cannot do this in myself, but in Thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He acknowledged what he had of himself, and what of Him, by whose will he believed that he could do that, which no human weakness could do.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore, “if it be Thou, bid me;” because when thou biddest, it will be done.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What I cannot do by taking it upon myself, Thou canst do by bidding me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And the Lord said “Come.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And without any doubting, at the word of Him who bade him, at the presence of Him who sustained, at the presence of Him who guided him, without any delay, Peter leaped down into the water, and began to walk.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He was able to do what the Lord was doing, not in himself, but in the Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For ye were sometimes darkness, but now are ye light in the Lord.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What no one can do in Paul, no one in Peter, no one in any other of the Apostles, this can he do in the Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore well said Paul by a wholesome despising of himself, and commending of Him; “Was Paul crucified for you, or were ye baptized in the name of Paul?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then, ye are not in me, but together with me; not under me, but under Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore Peter walked on the water by the bidding of the Lord, knowing that he could not have this power of himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By faith he had strength to do what human weakness could not do.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These are the strong ones of the Church.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Mark this, hear, understand, and act accordingly.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For we must not deal with the strong on any other principle than this, that so they should become weak; but thus we must deal with the weak, that they may become strong.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the presuming on their own strength keeps many back from strength.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No one will have strength from God, but he who feels himself weak of himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “God setteth apart a spontaneous rain for His inheritance.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why do you, who know what I was about to say, anticipate me?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let your quickness be moderated, that the slowness of the rest may follow.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This I said, and I say it again; hear it, receive it, and act on this principle.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No one is made strong by God, but he who feels himself weak of his own self.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And therefore a “spontaneous rain,” as the Psalm says, “spontaneous;” not of our deserts, but “spontaneous.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “A spontaneous rain” therefore “God setteth apart for his inheritance;” for “it was weak; but Thou hast perfected it.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because Thou “hast set apart for it a spontaneous rain,” not looking to men’s deserts, but to Thine own grace and mercy.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This inheritance then was weakened, and acknowledged its own weakness in itself, that it might be strong in Thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It would not be strengthened, if it were not weak, that by Thee it might be “perfected” in Thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See Paul a small portion of this inheritance, see him in weakness, who said, “I am not meet to be called an Apostle, because I persecuted the Church of God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why then art thou an Apostle?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “By the grace of God I am what I am.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I am not meet, but by the grace of God I am what I am.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Paul was “weak,” but Thou hast “perfected” him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But now because by “the grace of God he is what he is,” look what follows; “And His grace in me was not in vain, but I laboured more abundantly than they all.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Take heed lest thou lose by presumption what thou hast attained through weakness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is well, very well; that “I am not meet to be called an Apostle.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By His grace I am what I am, and His grace in me was not in vain:” all most excellent.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But, “I laboured more abundantly than they all;” thou hast begun, it would seem, to ascribe to thyself what a little before thou hadst given to God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Attend and follow on; “Yet not I, but the grace of God with me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Well! thou weak one; thou shalt be exalted in exceeding strength, seeing thou art not unthankful.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou art the very same Paul, little in thyself; and great in the Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou art he who didst thrice beseech the Lord, that “the thorn of the flesh, the messenger of Satan, by whom thou wast buffeted, might be taken away from thee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what was said to thee? what didst thou hear when thou madest this petition?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “My grace is sufficient for thee: for My strength is made perfect in weakness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he was “weak,” but Thou didst “perfect” him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So Peter also said, “Bid me come unto Thee on the water.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I who dare this am but a man, but it is no man whom I beseech.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let the God-man bid, that man may be able to do what man cannot do.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Come,” said He.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And He went down, and began to walk on the water; and Peter was able, because the Rock had bidden him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, what Peter was in the Lord; what was he in himself?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “When he saw the wind boisterous, he was afraid; and beginning to sink, he cried out, Lord, I perish, save me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When he looked for strength from the Lord, he had strength from the Lord; as a man he tottered, but he returned to the Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “If I said, my foot hath slipped” (they are the words of a Psalm, the notes of a holy song; and if we acknowledge them they are our words too; yea, if we will, they are ours also).

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “If I said my foot hath slipped.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How slipped, except because it was mine own.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what follows?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thy mercy, Lord, helped me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Not mine own strength, but Thy mercy.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For will God forsake him as he totters, whom He heard when calling upon Him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Where then is that, “Who hath called upon God, and hath been forsaken by Him?” where again is that, “Whosoever shall call on the Name of the Lord, shall be delivered.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Immediately reaching forth the help of His right hand, He lifted him up as he was sinking, and rebuked his distrust; “O thou of little faith, wherefore didst thou doubt?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Once thou didst trust in Me, hast thou now doubted of Me?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Well, brethren, my sermon must be ended.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Consider the world to be the sea; the wind is boisterous, and there is a mighty tempest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Each man’s peculiar lust is his tempest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou dost love God; thou walkest upon the sea, and under thy feet is the swelling of the world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou dost love the world, it will swallow thee up.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It skilleth only how to devour its lovers, not to carry them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But when thy heart is tossed about by lust, in order that thou mayest get the better of thy lust, call upon the Divinity of Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Think ye that the wind is then contrary, when there is this life’s adversity?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For so when there are wars, when there is tumult, when there is famine, when there is pestilence, when even to every individual man his private calamity arriveth, then the wind is thought to be contrary, then it is thought that God must be called upon.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But when the world wears her smile of temporal happiness, it is as if there were no contrary wind.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But do not ask upon this matter the tranquil state of the times: ask only your own lust.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See if there be tranquillity within thee: see if there be no inner wind which overturns thee; see to this.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There needs great virtue to struggle with happiness, lest this very happiness allure, corrupt, and overthrow thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There needs, I say, great virtue to struggle with happiness, and great happiness not to be overcome by happiness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Learn then to tread upon the world; remember to trust in Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And “if thy foot have slipped;” if thou totter, if some things there are which thou canst not overcome, if thou begin to sink, say, “Lord, I perish, save me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Say, “I perish,” that thou perish not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He only can deliver thee from the death of the body, who died in the body for thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us turn to the Lord, etc.
